Title: Total biosynthesis of the cyclic AMP booster forskolin from Coleus forskohlii

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in root cork transcriptome; the only POR identified there
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed in engineered yeast to support P450 activity
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports P450-dependent oxidation steps necessary for forskolin biosynthesis in yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfPOR was identified from the root cork transcriptome as the only POR present there. 2. It was required as reductase partner to support the activity of the forskolin-pathway P450s in yeast. 3. Co-integration with pathway genes enabled heterologous forskolin production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; cloning; co-integration in yeast; metabolite profiling of transformed strains
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important support component for microbial reconstruction of plant P450-rich diterpenoid pathways; interaction optimization may improve yields
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not itself shown to alter metabolite structure directly, but required for electron transfer to P450s

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in transiently transformed N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acetylates deacetylforskolin but with low regioselective specificity, yielding forskolin as a minor product plus side products
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfACT1-6 acetylated deacetylforskolin in planta. 2. It generated a broad range of acetylated products and only a minor fraction of forskolin. 3. It was inferior to CfACT1-8 for specific forskolin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana producing deacetylforskolin; LC-MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less suitable for precise forskolin engineering, but may be useful for generating acetylated diterpenoid diversity
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was functionally validated in Results as an alternative acetyltransferase affecting forskolin-related product profile

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently active in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts 13R-manoyl oxide mainly to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and also forms 1,11-dihydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide; can substitute partially in deacetylforskolin formation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfCYP76AH8 converted 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and additional multi-oxygenated products including 1,11-dihydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide. 2. Its oxygenation pattern is compatible with forskolin biosynthesis but less specific than CfCYP76AH15. 3. In combination with CfCYP76AH11 and CfCYP76AH16 it could also support deacetylforskolin formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; qRT-PCR; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; LC-qTOF-MS; NMR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broader product spectrum and lower specificity than CfCYP76AH15
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful alternative/promiscuous P450 for combinatorial diterpenoid engineering
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core but not part of the minimal most specific forskolin pathway

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently active in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts 13R-manoyl oxide mainly to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide and 1,11-dihydroxy intermediates; can support deacetylforskolin formation with other CYPs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfCYP76AH17 catalyzed formation of 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide as a major product. 2. It also produced 1,11-dihydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide and other higher oxygenated derivatives. 3. Together with CfCYP76AH11 and CfCYP76AH16 it supported deacetylforskolin formation, though less optimally than CfCYP76AH15.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; qRT-PCR; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; LC-qTOF-MS; NMR
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity and broader oxygenation profile reduce pathway specificity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative P450 for combinatorial reconstruction of oxygenated labdane diterpenoids
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core but not selected as the optimal C-11 oxidase for minimal pathway

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 13R-manoyl oxide mainly at C-11 to form 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide; also oxidizes miltiradiene/dehydroabietadiene route to ferruginol.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate/precursor required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently active in N. benthamiana leaves; functional after stable expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes forskolin biosynthesis by efficiently introducing the C-11 keto functionality from 13R-manoyl oxide; part of minimal three-P450 set producing deacetylforskolin/forskolin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfCYP76AH15 efficiently and specifically converted 13R-manoyl oxide to 11-oxo-13R-manoyl oxide with highest efficiency among tested CYP76AHs. 2. Together with CfCYP76AH11 and CfCYP76AH16 it gave the highest deacetylforskolin formation and supported forskolin production. 3. It also converted miltiradiene to ferruginol, showing multifunctionality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; qRT-PCR expression analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; LC-qTOF-MS; NMR for intermediates; stable pathway integration in yeast; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves; glucose-limited fermentation in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; discussion suggests one or more P450 steps limit flux in yeast, but not assigned specifically to CfCYP76AH15 alone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In yeast, incomplete conversion caused accumulation of 13R-manoyl oxide and 9-hydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide, indicating flux imbalance through P450 steps
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme for reconstructing forskolin pathway; useful for combinatorial diterpenoid engineering and mixed-species P450 panels; optimization of expression/kinetics in yeast recommended
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also likely involved in ferruginol biosynthesis in planta; functionally similar to rosemary CYP76AH4

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces hydroxylations mainly at C-1, C-6 and C-7 on 13R-manoyl oxide-derived intermediates, producing multi-oxygenated intermediates including 9-deoxydeacetylforskolin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor pattern toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently active in N. benthamiana leaves; functional after stable expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides complementary hydroxylations required for deacetylforskolin/forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfCYP76AH11 generated monooxygenated and multi-oxygenated 13R-manoyl oxide derivatives including 9-deoxydeacetylforskolin. 2. Combined with CfCYP76AH15 and CfCYP76AH16 it enabled highest deacetylforskolin production. 3. It mainly accounts for C-1, C-6 and C-7 oxygenation pattern in forskolin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; qRT-PCR expression analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; LC-qTOF-MS; NMR of pathway intermediates; stable pathway integration in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves; glucose-limited fermentation in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: P450 flux imbalance in yeast may contribute to intermediate buildup and incomplete forskolin formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential member of minimal P450 set for heterologous forskolin production; target for optimization of expression and catalytic efficiency in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shows multifunctionality and partial redundancy with other CYP76AH enzymes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 13R-manoyl oxide at C-9 to form 9-hydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide (coleorol), providing the C-9 oxygenation present in forskolin.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate/precursor required for target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly or exclusively expressed in root cork cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently active in N. benthamiana leaves; functional after stable expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Exclusively provides C-9 hydroxylation required for forskolin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfCYP76AH16 predominantly produced 9-hydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide from 13R-manoyl oxide. 2. It was the only enzyme reported to catalyze monooxygenation at C-9. 3. Together with CfCYP76AH15 and CfCYP76AH11 it gave the highest deacetylforskolin production and supported forskolin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; 5'RACE cloning; qRT-PCR expression analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS; LC-qTOF-MS; NMR; stable pathway integration in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves; glucose-limited fermentation in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; 9-hydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide accumulated strongly in yeast, implying downstream/overall P450 limitation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High accumulation of 9-hydroxy-13R-manoyl oxide in yeast indicates incomplete downstream conversion
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential P450 for complete heterologous forskolin pathway; key target for balancing pathway flux in microbial production
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes regiospecific acetylation of the C-7 hydroxyl group of deacetylforskolin using acetyl-CoA to form forskolin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cyclic AMP booster used in treatment of glaucoma and heart failure
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functional in N. benthamiana leaves and in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Efficiently and specifically converts deacetylforskolin to forskolin with minimal side products
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CfACT1-8 acetylated deacetylforskolin to forskolin. 2. It showed high activity and specificity, unlike CfACT1-6 which produced multiple acetylated side products. 3. Inclusion of CfACT1-8 completed the entire biosynthetic pathway and enabled forskolin production in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Root cork RNA-seq candidate mining; 5'RACE cloning; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana producing deacetylforskolin; LC-MS; comparison with authentic forskolin standard; stable pathway integration in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana leaves; glucose-limited fermentation in S. cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for forskolin formation but not limiting in yeast according to low deacetylforskolin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Few detectable side products compared with CfACT1-6; no major penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preferred terminal enzyme for precise metabolic engineering of forskolin; superior to CfACT1-6 for specific product formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root cork RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SAMN06013363
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to clade III BAHDs, distinct from taxol-related clade V diterpenoid acyltransferases
